summary: This notebook demonstrates a multi-model baseline for a community rule violation detection task by combining fine-tuned transformers, semantic search, and large language model inference. It flattens positive and negative example columns to expand the training set, extracts URL semantics via regex, and leverages round-trip translation to augment rule variants. The pipeline trains DeBERTa-v3 and DistilRoBERTa on flattened examples, scores predictions using a LoRA-finetuned Qwen3 embedding model, and runs few-shot classification with a Qwen2.5-14B instruct model via vLLM. Final predictions are combined through rank-based weighted blending to mitigate model-specific biases. Readers should learn how to effectively augment sparse example data, integrate URL context, use semantic search for scoring, and apply rank-based ensembling for robust baselines.
competition_flow: Loads train/test CSVs, flattens example columns into a unified dataset, extracts URL semantics, trains/fines-tunes DeBERTa-v3, DistilRoBERTa, and Qwen3 embeddings, runs inference with Qwen2.5-14B via vLLM, and combines all five prediction files using rank-based weighted averaging to produce a final submission.
data_reading: Flattens positive_example_1/2 and negative_example_1/2 columns into a single body column with corresponding rule_violation labels (1 or 0)
data_processing: Flattens example columns, drops duplicates on body/rule/subreddit, shuffles data
Extracts URL domain/path keywords via regex and appends to text
Cleans text with cleantext (fix_unicode, to_ascii, remove URLs/emails/phones)
Maps labels to 1/-1 for semantic search
features_engineering: Round-trip translation of rules (en -> fr/de/es -> en) to create synthetic rule variants
URL semantics extraction (domain and path keywords)
cv_strategy: None (commented out; uses full training data)
ensembling: Rank-based weighted blending of five model submissions (0.5 DeBERTa, 0.1 Qwen3-Embedding, 0.1 DistilRoBERTa, 0.1 Qwen2.5-14B, 0.2 DeBERTa-AUC) to produce the final submission.
insights: Flattening positive and negative example columns into a unified training set effectively augments the dataset without manual feature engineering.
Extracting URL domain and path keywords via regex provides valuable contextual signals for rule violation detection.
Rank-based weighted blending is a robust strategy for combining heterogeneous model predictions compared to direct probability averaging.
Fine-tuning a lightweight embedding model with LoRA enables efficient semantic search-based scoring as a strong baseline.
Using vLLM with a logits processor allows efficient few-shot classification with large instruct models without full fine-tuning.
